{"step_by_step_thinking": "Bobble head doll syndrome is described in the MEDCORP document as a rhythmic movement of the head due to third ventricular dilation and potentially cerebellar involvement. Third ventricular dilation is a finding that can be associated with hydrocephalus, as hydrocephalus involves the dilation of the ventricles due to increased intraventricular pressure. The document explicitly links bobble-head doll syndrome to third ventricular dilation, which is a key feature of hydrocephalus. Although the term 'hydrocephalus' is not directly used in the description, the pathophysiological basis—third ventricular dilation—is a hallmark of hydrocephalus. Therefore, the syndrome is associated with hydrocephalus through this anatomical and pathological connection.", "answer_choice": "A"}
